seoTitle: ESIONTECH FPGA Products | Programmable Logic | LiTong Distributor
seoDescription: Browse ESIONTECH FPGA products including low-cost, high-performance, and industrial-grade programmable logic devices. Technical specifications available.

faqs.question: What FPGA densities does ESIONTECH offer?
faqs.answer: ESIONTECH offers a wide range of FPGA densities: (1) Low-Cost Series - 1K to 10K LUTs for simple logic and control applications. (2) Mid-Range Series - 10K to 30K LUTs for moderate complexity designs. (3) High-Performance Series - 30K to 100K+ LUTs for complex signal processing and communications. (4) Logic Elements - Each LUT typically implements one logic function with associated flip-flop. (5) DSP Blocks - Dedicated hardware multipliers and accumulators for signal processing. (6) Memory - Block RAM ranging from kilobits to megabits. The density selection depends on your design complexity, including logic requirements, memory needs, and DSP processing. LiTong can help estimate optimal density for your application.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong for density selection and FPGA sizing analysis.

faqs.question: How do I select the right ESIONTECH FPGA for my application?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right ESIONTECH FPGA involves several considerations: (1) Logic Requirements - Estimate LUT count based on your design. Use vendor tools for accurate estimation. (2) I/O Count - Determine number of pins needed including user I/O, clock inputs, and configuration pins. (3) Memory - Calculate internal RAM requirements and external memory interface needs. (4) DSP - Count DSP blocks needed for multipliers and MAC operations. (5) Speed - Select speed grade based on maximum clock frequency requirements. (6) Power - Consider static and dynamic power consumption. (7) Package - Choose based on pin count and board space. (8) Cost - Balance features with budget. LiTong FAEs can analyze your requirements and recommend optimal ESIONTECH solutions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong for personalized FPGA selection assistance.

faqs.question: What is the difference between low-cost and high-performance FPGA?
faqs.answer: ESIONTECH offers different performance tiers: (1) Low-Cost FPGA - Optimized for cost-sensitive applications. Fewer LUTs, basic I/O, lower power, simpler architecture. Ideal for consumer, IoT, and simple control. (2) High-Performance FPGA - Advanced features including high-speed transceivers, more DSP blocks, larger memory, and higher logic density. Target communications, video, and complex processing. (3) Key Differences: Logic capacity, I/O speed and features, DSP resources, memory amount, power consumption, and price. (4) Selection - Choose low-cost for simple designs with tight budgets. Choose high-performance for demanding applications requiring speed and capacity. Many systems use both types for different functions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong for performance analysis and product selection.

faqs.question: Does ESIONTECH support standard FPGA design flows?
faqs.answer: Yes, ESIONTECH FPGA supports industry-standard design flows: (1) Design Entry - Verilog, VHDL, and schematic capture supported. Mixed-language designs accepted. (2) Synthesis - Integrated synthesis tool optimizes logic for ESIONTECH architecture. (3) Placement and Routing - Automatic placement and routing with timing-driven optimization. (4) Simulation - Functional and timing simulation with waveform viewing. (5) Debugging - On-chip logic analyzer and signal probing capabilities. (6) IP Integration - Standard IP core interfaces for easy integration. (7) Constraints - Industry-standard SDC timing constraints supported. (8) Output - Standard bitstream format for device configuration. The design flow is similar to other FPGA vendors, making migration straightforward. LiTong provides design flow training and support.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong for design flow guidance and tool training.

faqs.question: What IP cores are available for ESIONTECH FPGA?
faqs.answer: ESIONTECH provides a rich library of IP cores: (1) Interface IP - PCIe, Ethernet, USB, DDR memory controllers, SPI, I2C, UART. (2) Processing - Soft processor cores, DSP functions, mathematical operations. (3) Video - Video processing, image processing, display controllers. (4) Communications - Modulation, demodulation, encoding, decoding functions. (5) Memory - FIFOs, RAM controllers, cache controllers. (6) Standard Functions - Counters, timers, PWM generators, encoders. (7) Security - Encryption, decryption, authentication cores. (8) Custom IP - ESIONTECH can develop custom IP for specific applications. Most IP cores are provided as encrypted RTL with user documentation. LiTong can help select and integrate appropriate IP cores for your design.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong for IP core selection and integration support.

categories.name: Low-Cost FPGA
categories.description: Cost-effective FPGA solutions for consumer electronics, IoT, and simple industrial control applications with optimized power consumption.
categories.longDescription: ESIONTECH Low-Cost FPGA series provides cost-effective programmable logic solutions for price-sensitive applications. These devices offer essential FPGA functionality with optimized power consumption and small form factors. With logic capacities ranging from 1K to 10K LUTs, the low-cost series is ideal for consumer electronics, IoT devices, simple industrial control, and basic signal processing. Key features include low static power, flexible I/O options, and integrated oscillators. The devices support standard FPGA design flows using Verilog and VHDL. Development tools include free license options for cost-sensitive projects. As an authorized ESIONTECH distributor, LiTong provides comprehensive product selection guidance and technical support for low-cost FPGA applications.

series.name: ES1K Series
series.description: Entry-level FPGA with 1K-4K LUTs for simple logic applications

series.name: ES10K Series
series.description: Mid-range low-cost FPGA with 4K-10K LUTs for moderate complexity

selectionGuideLink.text: View Low-Cost FPGA Selection Guide for detailed selection guidance

faqs.question: What densities are available in ESIONTECH Low-Cost FPGA?
faqs.answer: ESIONTECH Low-Cost FPGA densities: (1) ES1K Series - 1K to 4K LUTs for simple logic functions, basic state machines, and simple interfaces. (2) ES10K Series - 4K to 10K LUTs for moderate complexity including small processors, interface bridges, and control logic. (3) I/O Options - 20 to 100+ user I/O pins depending on device and package. (4) Memory - Up to 100KB block RAM for data buffering and FIFOs. (5) DSP - Basic DSP slices for simple multiplications. Select density based on logic complexity, with 20-30% margin for future expansion. LiTong can help estimate LUT requirements for your design.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong for density selection and logic estimation.

faqs.question: What is the power consumption of Low-Cost FPGA?
faqs.answer: ESIONTECH Low-Cost FPGA power consumption: (1) Static Power - Typically 5-20mW depending on device size and temperature. Very low for battery-powered applications. (2) Dynamic Power - Depends on design activity and clock frequency. Typically 10-50mW at moderate frequencies. (3) I/O Power - Depends on I/O standards, voltage levels, and switching activity. (4) Total Power - Usually 20-100mW for typical designs. (5) Power Savings - Devices support power-down modes and clock gating. (6) Thermal - Low power eliminates need for heatsinks in most applications. For battery-powered designs, low-cost FPGA offers excellent power efficiency. LiTong can provide power analysis for your specific design.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong for power analysis and thermal design.

faqs.question: What packages are available for Low-Cost FPGA?
faqs.answer: ESIONTECH Low-Cost FPGA package options: (1) QFN-32/48/64 - Small leadless packages for space-constrained designs. Good for consumer and IoT. (2) TQFP-48/64/100 - Thin quad flat pack for industrial applications. Easy for assembly and rework. (3) CSP - Chip scale package for smallest footprint. Ideal for wearables. (4) Package Selection - Based on I/O count, board space, thermal requirements, and cost. (5) Migration - Same devices available in multiple packages for design flexibility. Low-cost FPGA packages are optimized for cost and size while maintaining good thermal performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong for package selection and PCB layout guidance.

faqs.question: Does Low-Cost FPGA support embedded processors?
faqs.answer: ESIONTECH Low-Cost FPGA supports soft processors: (1) Soft Cores - RISC-V and proprietary soft processor cores can be implemented in logic. (2) Resource Usage - Small processors use 1K-3K LUTs, feasible in larger low-cost devices. (3) Performance - Typical operation at 50-100MHz depending on processor complexity. (4) Peripherals - UART, SPI, I2C, timers can be implemented in remaining logic. (5) Memory - Use block RAM for program and data memory. External memory for larger programs. (6) Tools - Standard development tools support soft processor design and debugging. For processor-intensive applications, consider larger FPGA or dedicated microcontrollers. LiTong can advise on processor implementation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong for soft processor implementation guidance.

faqs.question: What development tools support Low-Cost FPGA?
faqs.answer: ESIONTECH Low-Cost FPGA development tools: (1) ES-Designer - Free development tool with synthesis, placement and routing, and timing analysis. (2) Simulation - Integrated simulator for functional verification. (3) Programming - Device programmer and configuration tools included. (4) IP Library - Basic IP cores for common functions provided free. (5) Documentation - Complete user manuals and tutorials. (6) License - Free license for low-cost devices with no restrictions. (7) Support - LiTong provides tool installation and usage support. The free tool chain makes low-cost FPGA ideal for learning, prototyping, and cost-sensitive production designs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong for development tool download and training.

products.name: ES4K-LP 4K LUT Low-Power FPGA
products.shortDescription: 4K LUT low-power FPGA with 80 I/O pins, optimized for consumer and IoT applications requiring minimal power consumption.
descriptionParagraphs: The ES4K-LP is a 4K LUT low-power FPGA designed for cost-sensitive and battery-powered applications. It features 80 user I/O pins and ultra-low static power consumption of less than 10mW.

The device includes 36KB of block RAM for data buffering and FIFO implementations. It supports various I/O standards including LVCMOS, LVTTL, and SSTL, making it versatile for different interface requirements.

With its small QFN-64 package and free development tools, the ES4K-LP is ideal for consumer electronics, IoT devices, portable equipment, and simple industrial control applications where cost and power are critical.

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - FPGA Products
faeReview.content: The ES4K-LP is an excellent entry-level FPGA for cost-sensitive designs. The 4K LUTs provide enough logic for many IoT and consumer applications. I've used this part in smart home devices and wearable products with great results. The sub-10mW static power is impressive - it's one of the lowest in its class. Key advantages: free development tools, low power, and competitive pricing. The QFN-64 package is compact but still hand-solderable for prototypes. Design tips: Use the integrated oscillator to save BOM cost. Enable power-down modes when logic is idle. The 36KB RAM is sufficient for small buffers and state machines. For simple designs, this FPGA often replaces MCUs with better parallel processing. Overall, outstanding value for low-complexity applications.
faeReview.highlight: Ultra-low power 4K LUT FPGA with free tools for cost-sensitive applications

alternativeParts.comparison: LUTs => 1K < 4K (smaller); I/O => 40 < 80; cost => lower price; applications => simpler designs
alternativeParts.reason: Lower cost for very simple logic applications

alternativeParts.comparison: LUTs => 10K > 4K (larger); I/O => 120 > 80; cost => higher price; applications => complex designs
alternativeParts.reason: Higher capacity for more complex designs

faqs.question: What can I implement with 4K LUTs?
faqs.answer: With 4K LUTs in ES4K-LP, you can implement: (1) Interface Controllers - SPI, I2C, UART, simple USB. (2) Control Logic - State machines, sequencers, simple processors. (3) Signal Processing - Basic filtering, simple DSP functions. (4) Display Control - LED matrix drivers, simple LCD controllers. (5) Communication - Simple protocol converters, data formatters. (6) Motor Control - Basic PWM, simple servo control. Typical designs use 2K-3K LUTs, leaving margin for expansion. Complex processors or video processing may need larger devices.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong for logic capacity planning.

faqs.question: How do I program the ES4K-LP?
faqs.answer: Programming ES4K-LP: (1) Configuration - Device uses SRAM-based configuration loaded at power-up. (2) Configuration Modes - Supports JTAG, SPI master, and SPI slave modes. (3) External Flash - Store configuration in external SPI flash for automatic loading. (4) Programming Tools - Use ES-Designer software to generate bitstream. (5) Programmer - Use USB-JTAG cable or integrated programmer. (6) In-System - Program device while mounted on PCB via JTAG. (7) Updates - Reconfigure device without hardware changes. The flexible configuration options support various system architectures.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong for programming procedures and tools.

faqs.question: What is the maximum clock frequency?
faqs.answer: ES4K-LP clock frequency: (1) Internal Logic - Typical designs run at 50-100MHz. Maximum depends on design complexity and timing constraints. (2) I/O Speed - Supports up to 200MHz for high-speed interfaces. (3) Global Clocks - 8 global clock networks with low skew. (4) PLL - Integrated PLL for clock multiplication and phase shifting. (5) Performance - Sufficient for most control and interface applications. (6) Timing - Use timing constraints to optimize critical paths. For higher performance, consider ESIONTECH high-performance series.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong for timing analysis and optimization.

faqs.question: Can I use external memory with ES4K-LP?
faqs.answer: External memory support: (1) SPI Flash - For configuration storage and data. Standard SPI interface. (2) SRAM - Parallel SRAM interface using I/O pins. (3) DDR - Simple DDR support for larger memory needs. (4) Interface - Implement memory controllers in FPGA logic. (5) Bandwidth - Limited by I/O pin count and speed. (6) Use Cases - Program storage, data buffering, lookup tables. The 36KB internal RAM handles small buffers; external memory extends capacity for larger applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong for memory interface design.

faqs.question: What I/O standards are supported?
faqs.answer: ES4K-LP I/O standards: (1) Single-Ended - LVCMOS 3.3V, 2.5V, 1.8V; LVTTL; SSTL for memory. (2) Differential - LVDS for high-speed signaling. (3) Configurable - Each bank supports different voltages. (4) Drive Strength - Programmable drive strength and slew rate. (5) Protection - ESD protection on all I/O pins. (6) Standards - Supports most common digital interfaces. Check datasheet for complete list. The flexible I/O supports interfacing with various devices and systems.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong for I/O planning and interface design.

products.name: ES10K-LP 10K LUT Low-Power FPGA
products.shortDescription: 10K LUT low-power FPGA with 120 I/O pins and 90KB RAM for moderate complexity designs in consumer and industrial appl...
descriptionParagraphs: The ES10K-LP is a 10K LUT low-power FPGA designed for moderate complexity applications requiring more logic resources. It features 120 user I/O pins and 90KB of block RAM.
The device includes 20 DSP blocks for signal processing applications and supports various high-speed I/O standards. It offers excellent logic density for cost-sensitive designs.
With its TQFP-100 package and comprehensive development tools, the ES10K-LP is ideal for industrial control, communications interfaces, video processing, and complex IoT applications.

faeReview.author: Sarah Liu
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - FPGA Applications
faeReview.content: The ES10K-LP hits the sweet spot for many industrial and communications applications. The 10K LUTs handle complex control logic and interface bridging. I've used this in protocol converters, industrial controllers, and video processing with excellent results. The 90KB RAM is generous for buffering and lookup tables. Key advantages: good logic density, plenty of I/O, and reasonable power. The TQFP package is easy to work with for both prototyping and production. Design tips: Use DSP blocks for filtering rather than implementing in logic. Plan I/O banks for different voltage domains. The integrated PLL saves external clock chips. For designs that outgrow small FPGAs but don't need high-end features, this is the perfect choice.
faeReview.highlight: 10K LUT FPGA with rich features for moderate complexity designs

alternativeParts.comparison: LUTs => 4K < 10K (smaller); I/O => 80 < 120; cost => lower price; applications => simpler designs
alternativeParts.reason: Lower cost for designs with less logic requirements

alternativeParts.comparison: LUTs => 20K > 10K (larger); features => high-speed transceivers; cost => higher price; applications => high-performance

faqs.question: What designs fit in 10K LUTs?
faqs.answer: 10K LUTs in ES10K-LP supports: (1) Soft Processors - Complete RISC-V or ARM Cortex-M0 soft cores. (2) Complex Interfaces - Multi-protocol bridges, USB controllers. (3) Video - Simple video processing, format conversion. (4) Communications - Protocol stacks, modems, encoders. (5) Control - Multi-axis motor control, complex state machines. (6) Signal Processing - FIR filters, FFTs using DSP blocks. This capacity handles most industrial and consumer applications. Large SoC designs or complex video processing may need high-performance series.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong for design capacity planning.

faqs.question: How many soft processors can I fit?
faqs.answer: Soft processor capacity: (1) RISC-V - One complete RV32I core uses ~2K-3K LUTs. Can fit 2-3 cores in ES10K-LP. (2) ARM Cortex-M0 - Soft implementation ~3K-4K LUTs. One core comfortably fits. (3) Custom Processors - Optimized cores may use less logic. (4) Peripherals - Additional logic needed for UART, timers, etc. (5) Performance - Soft cores typically run 50-100MHz. (6) Use Cases - Control processors, protocol handling, state machines. The 10K LUTs provide good capacity for processor-based designs with additional logic.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong for soft processor implementation.

faqs.question: What video processing can I do?
faqs.answer: Video processing capabilities: (1) Format Conversion - Color space conversion, resolution scaling. (2) Overlay - Text and graphics overlay on video. (3) Filtering - Simple noise reduction, edge enhancement using DSP blocks. (4) Interfaces - VGA, DVI, HDMI (lower resolution). (5) Limitations - 10K LUTs support SD/HD video but not 4K. Complex codecs need larger devices. (6) Bandwidth - Depends on I/O pin availability and speed. Good for industrial cameras, displays, and simple video systems.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong for video processing design.

faqs.question: Can I implement Ethernet in ES10K-LP?
faqs.answer: Ethernet implementation: (1) 10/100 Ethernet - MAC layer can be implemented in logic. PHY external or use FPGA I/O. (2) MAC Core - Soft Ethernet MAC uses ~2K-3K LUTs. (3) Protocol Stack - TCP/IP stack needs processor + memory. (4) Bandwidth - 100Mbps achievable with proper design. (5) IP Cores - ESIONTECH provides Ethernet MAC IP. (6) Limitations - Gigabit Ethernet needs high-performance series. Good for industrial Ethernet, IoT gateways, and embedded networking.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong for Ethernet implementation guidance.

faqs.question: What debugging features are available?
faqs.answer: ES10K-LP debugging features: (1) JTAG - Standard JTAG interface for boundary scan and programming. (2) Logic Analyzer - Embedded logic analyzer cores for signal capture. (3) Signal Probe - Route internal signals to I/O pins for external scope. (4) Simulation - Functional and timing simulation in ES-Designer. (5) Timing Analysis - Static timing analysis with path reporting. (6) On-Chip Debug - Soft processor debug interfaces. The comprehensive debugging capabilities help identify and resolve issues quickly.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong for debugging tool usage.

products.name: ES1K 10K LUTs Low-Cost FPGA
products.shortDescription: Low-cost FPGA with 10K LUTs, 256KB RAM, 80 I/O pins for industrial and consumer applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The ES1K-10K32 is a cost-effective FPGA from ESIONTECH's ES1K series, designed for industrial control, consumer electronics, and IoT applications.

With 10,240 LUTs, 256KB block RAM, and 80 user I/O pins, this FPGA provides sufficient resources for motor control, protocol conversion, and simple video processing. The device operates from a single 3.3V supply with 1.2V core voltage.

Features include low static power consumption (<100mW), wide temperature range (-40°C to +85°C), and support for multiple I/O standards including LVCMOS, LVTTL, and SSTL. The TQFP-144 package is ideal for cost-sensitive applications.

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - FPGA Applications
faeReview.content: The ES1K-10K32 is an excellent choice for cost-sensitive FPGA applications. The 10K LUTs provide sufficient resources for most industrial control tasks. I've successfully used this FPGA in motor control and protocol bridge applications. The TQFP package is easy to solder and debug. The low power consumption is ideal for battery-powered applications. The development tools are mature and easy to use. For applications requiring more resources, consider the ES1K-20K64.

faqs.question: What development tools are available for ES1K-10K32?
faqs.answer: ESIONTECH provides the ES-Design Suite including synthesis, placement and routing, and simulation tools. The software supports Verilog and VHDL. A free license is available for the ES1K series. Programming is done via JTAG interface using the ES-Programmer.
faqs.decisionGuide: Download ES-Design Suite from ESIONTECH website.

products.name: ES1K 20K LUTs Low-Cost FPGA
products.shortDescription: Higher capacity low-cost FPGA with 20K LUTs, 512KB RAM, 120 I/O pins for complex applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The ES1K-20K64 is a higher capacity low-cost FPGA from ESIONTECH's ES1K series, designed for more complex industrial and consumer applications.

With 20,480 LUTs, 512KB block RAM, and 120 user I/O pins, this FPGA handles complex control algorithms, multi-protocol interfaces, and video processing. The device maintains low power consumption while providing more resources.
Features include enhanced DSP blocks (40 total), support for DDR2/DDR3 memory interfaces, and industrial temperature range. The TQFP-176 package provides more I/O for complex designs.

faeReview.author: David Wang
faeReview.title: Principal FAE - FPGA Solutions
faeReview.content: The ES1K-20K64 provides excellent value for complex FPGA designs. The 20K LUTs handle sophisticated control algorithms that the 10K version cannot. I've used this FPGA in multi-axis motor controllers and video processing applications. The DDR memory interface support is valuable for frame buffering. The additional DSP blocks accelerate signal processing tasks. For designs that are too large for the 10K version, this is the natural upgrade.

faqs.question: Does ES1K-20K64 support external memory interfaces?
faqs.answer: Yes, the ES1K-20K64 supports DDR2 and DDR3 memory interfaces through hardened memory controllers. Maximum data rate is 800 Mbps for DDR3. The FPGA includes dedicated I/O banks for memory interfaces with calibrated delays.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use hardened memory controllers for DDR2/DDR3 interfaces.
